feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Best practice for multiple Reporting Entities in Statutory Report
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am working on an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition rollout and I would like to clarify the recommended approach for configuring Reporting Entities for statutory reporting.

The customer has two different legal entities (two different Company Codes), each with its own Tax Identification Number and Tax Office.

Example:

Company Code A
Portuguese legal entity with its own NIF
Tax Office: [Tax Office A]
Company Code B
Different Portuguese legal entity with a different NIF
Tax Office: [Tax Office B]

In the Statutory Reporting configuration, under:

Reporting Entity → Enter Parameters Specific to Reporting Entity

there are parameters such as PT_TA_CAE.

My questions are:

For two different legal entities with different NIFs, is the recommended approach to create two separate Reporting Entities (one per legal entity), or can both Company Codes be assigned to the same Reporting Entity with one Leading Organizational Unit?
If multiple Company Codes are assigned to the same Reporting Entity, how are entity-specific parameters such as PT_TA_OFFICE and Tax Identification Number handled?
Is there any SAP Best Practice or recommendation for localization when multiple legal entities are implemented in the same S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system?

Any guidance or experience from similar implementations would be highly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: PR Workflow Issue
entry.content:
We are planning to revise the existing workflow and implement the new approval process based on Document Type.

However, during the configuration of the Purchase Requisition (PR) Flexible Workflow, we observed that the Document Type field is not available in the workflow item-level start conditions in our system.

As the new approval matrix is proposed to be driven based on Document Type, we kindly request your support in verifying whether this field can be enabled or made available in our server for PR workflow configuration.

entry.title: How to automate MM period closing using the MMPV Close Period app - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
We would like to schedule the MM period close job (MMPV) to run automatically on a monthly basis in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
When creating a recurring job for the MMPV transaction directly, the period parameter is mandatory and remains fixed at the value entered at job creation time, it does not auto-increment for each subsequent monthly run.

Our questions:

  1. Is it possible to schedule the period close so the posting period is automatically determined each month?
  2. Does the "Use current date" flag in the scheduling options allow the system to determine the correct MM posting period automatically?
  3. If automatic determination is not supported, is manual maintenance of the period parameter the standard behavior?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: 信用管理咨询：在未超过信用总额度的情况下，如何基于客户“超期未清项”自动拦截交货单？
entry.content:
Document must be blocked for above 100,000.00 CNY.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Custom Account Assignment Category not worked with Item Category Enhanced
entry.content:
Currently, we are using custom account assignment categories for Cost center, project & Assets. It worked well with Blank Account assignment category but not worked with Item Category Enhanced (E) 

Combination item category E/account assignment category L not defined

Message no. ME066

entry.title: Availability of Application-Specific User Guides for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello

We would like to confirm whether SAP provides official application-specific user guides or operation manuals for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

We are aware that general application information, implementation details, business catalogs, and related scope items can be found in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library and SAP Help Portal.

However, we are looking for more detailed documentation for each individual Fiori application, including:

 

- Step-by-step instructions for using the application

- Descriptions of screen fields, buttons, and functions

- Typical business process examples

- Prerequisites and operational considerations

- Downloadable user manuals or PDF documents, if available

 

Could you please confirm the following?

 

1. Does SAP provide an official user guide or operation manual for each Fiori application in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

2. Where can these application-specific guides be accessed?

3. Are downloadable PDF manuals available, or are the guides provided only through the SAP Help Portal?

4. If separate application-specific manuals are not available, what is the recommended official source for preparing end-user training materials for each application?

 

Please provide the relevant SAP Help Portal links or other official documentation.

 

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Regarding ATP verification based on variant selection
entry.content:
Please tell me about Variant Configuration in an S/4HANA Public Cloud TDD environment.
We would like to dynamically perform an ATP check and provide a delivery date confirmation based on the characteristic values ​​selected on the variant configuration screen.
Is this possible in S/4HANA Cloud?
・Calculate the delivery date by checking stock, purchase requisitions, planned orders, purchase orders, production orders, etc., for the configuration items selected on the variant configuration screen.

entry.title: How to post Free of Charge Delivery (BDA) inventory consumption to an expense account instead of COG
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We are implementing SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and are currently using the standard Scope Item BDA – Free of Charge Delivery for free sample deliveries.

During testing we observed that the PGI generated from a Free of Charge Delivery (CBFD / CBXN) creates a material document with Movement Type 601, resulting in the standard accounting entry:

Debit: Cost of Goods Sold
Credit: Inventory

From a Finance perspective, our business expects free samples to be recognized as a Sample/Marketing Expense instead of Cost of Goods Sold, while still crediting Inventory.

Has anyone implemented a similar requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Specifically:

Is there any standard configuration (SSCUI/CBC) that allows different G/L account determination for this scenario?
Does CBFD or CBXN influence account determination during PGI?
What is the SAP-recommended approach for this business requirement?

Any experience or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

Hiram Treviño

entry.title: Minor difference * INR 10 cannot be allocated across company codes
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have posted a Down Payment of INR 1,200 in Company Code 1001. We now want to clear/pay this advance from our Parent Company Code 1000 in USD currency, where the equivalent amount is USD 13.72.

However, during the payment process, although we are paying USD 13.72, the actual advance amount being cleared is INR 1,200. When we simulate the payment transaction, the system throws the following error:

"Minor difference INR 10 cannot be allocated across company codes."

entry.title: Error in account determination of Real Estate
entry.content:
Good morning, We are encountering an issue with the configuration of movement types and their account determination. Specifically, I am attempting to post movement type IN00 using the "Contract-Valuation Posting" app. This movement type is linked to the account symbol "CESP AO". Account symbol "CESP AO" is mapped to account number 16054000. However, when I run the posting simulation, it incorrectly shows account 16051000. 

Regards,

Raffaele

entry.title: Error with bank statement import
entry.content:
We regularly receive the error with bank statement import, details are in the attached file.

Sequence BranchAndFinancialInstitutionIdentification4 (N00551199622) must occur at least 1 times.

entry.title: AddressLine1-8 formatting in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Form Templates
entry.content:
In our S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation, we are currently performing customization development for form templates.
I would like to consult with you regarding the output of address information on these forms.

I understand that the address values for each form template are contained within the fields AddressLine1 through AddressLine8 in the XML.

Additionally, after checking the SAP Help site,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/b86c68c2371c472796b6b54c9502ae97.html

I recognize that the output values and positions for AddressLine1 to AddressLine8 are determined by the following conditions, and that international/domestic addresses are output based on the address notation:

1. The length of the address is compressed based on priorities defined for each address and address type element.
2. Addresses are formatted based on current domestic and international guidelines:
・International address patterns of the Universal Postal Union (UPU)
・Regulations of each country
In this case, it is impossible to predict which AddressLine will contain the company name or specific address items.
For instance, if there is a customer requirement to make only the company name bold or increase its font size, or to separate the output positions of the company name and the address, we must identify the specific AddressLine for the company name.

Do you know any method to identify what value is stored in which AddressLine?
Also, if there is a way to achieve the above requirements using the AddressLine items, I would like to know.

Thank you.

entry.title: Batch characteristic to be transferred from outbound delivery to batch in Inbound delivery.
entry.content:
Requirement is to transfer one of the batch characteristics from Subcontract and Stock transfer Outbound delivery to new batch created in Inbound delivery.

Steps to reproduce:

  1. Create a Subcontract PO
  2. Create Outbound delivery (Batch with a characteristic maintained)
  3. Perform Post Goods Issue
  4. Create subcontracting challan
  5. Create Inbound delivery
  6. Maintain outbound delivery batch in inbound delivery "SC Components"
  7. Select line item and click on create batch
  8. Now we have to manually enter outbound delivery batch characteristic.

Requirement is to transfer one of the batch characteristics from Subcontract and Stock transfer Outbound delivery to new batch created in Inbound delivery.

Business want a batch characteristic called "Heat number" to be transferred from Raw material to FG product.

The "Heat number" characteristic value in Raw material's batch will be picked during outbound delivery creation (subcontract & STO process). The same characteristic value should be picked automatically in Inbound delivery.

entry.title: Custom Field in App: Currency Exchange Rates
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We have a legal requirement in Indonesia where we need to enter the local exchange rate reference number (KMK Number) based on their government's directive as per Direktorat Jenderal Strategi Ekonomi dan Fiskal - Kurs Pajak .

For example, for exchange rates effective from 8th July - 14 July, the KMK reference number is KMK Nomor 31/MK/EF.2/2026

However, when creating custom field, we have no clue which business context we could use for this App: Currency Exchange Rates.

Please advise.

Thank you.

Best regards,

KK Low

entry.title: Unable to Proceed on the WM Config in S4 Hana Public Cloud
entry.content: We are implementing Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 2602 using SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC).The organizational structure has been completed successfully.The following configuration has already been maintained:Warehouse Numbers created in CBCWarehouse Numbers assigned to the respective PlantWarehouse Numbers mapped to the Storage LocationOrganizational structure deployment completed successfullyNo deployment errors reportedHowever, when navigating to the Warehouse Management implementation activities (SSCUIs), none of the created warehouse numbers are available in the warehouse-specific configuration activities.As a result, we cannot continue the warehouse implementation because warehouse-dependent configuration cannot be maintained

entry.title: Supplier Payment Identification in Bank Statements for Consolidated APP Payments
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are currently processing supplier payments through the Automatic Payment Program (APP), where multiple supplier payments are grouped into a single payment batch and transmitted to the bank as a consolidated payment file. Consequently, the bank statement is received as a single batch entry rather than as individual payment transactions.

Our requirement is to understand whether SAP has any best-practice recommendations regarding the fields that should be populated for supplier payments in this scenario.

We have already reviewed the Reference and Payment Reference fields. However, these values are typically derived from the supplier invoice, and such information is not consistently available on all invoices. In addition, we are not planning to enable the Individual Payment option.

Ultimately, we are looking for a reliable identifier that can be passed through the payment process and returned on the bank statement, allowing us to determine which SAP payment document the bank statement entry relates to.

Any recommendations or best practices from SAP or from your implementation experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

Regards,
Naveen Kumar

entry.title: Standard Values Overview remains empty after assigning Standard Value Key to Work Center
entry.content:
The Standard Value Key has been configured in the configuration tenant and successfully transported to the Production tenant. The configuration is visible in the Display Configuration view, confirming that the transport was completed successfully.

However, when creating or maintaining a Work Center and assigning the same Standard Value Key, the corresponding standard values (parameters/activities) are not populated automatically in the Work Center. The Standard Value Key is available for selection, but after assigning it, the expected standard value fields remain blank.

We have verified that:

The Standard Value Key configuration exists in the Production tenant.
The transport has been successfully imported.
The Standard Value Key is visible in the configuration display

entry.title: Badi not found for check validation for batch with respect to storage location
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We have the following business requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud:

During Production Order Confirmation for an SFG material, the user enters both a storage location and a batch number for the produced material.

Business rule:

  • Storage Location SOR1 should only allow batches starting with SOR (e.g., SOR1001).
  • Storage Location SIL1 should only allow batches starting with SIL (e.g., SIL1001).

Example:

  • Storage Location = SOR1

  • Batch = SOR1001 → Allowed

  • Storage Location = SOR1

  • Batch = SIL1001 → System should issue an error message and prevent saving the confirmation.

We are looking for a suitable BAdI  that can be triggered during Production Order Confirmation to validate the combination of storage location and batch number before the confirmation is saved.

Could you please advise which released BAdI can be used for this requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Regards, 
Name-Sagar Nagose

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | Restriction Field: User Group
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I create a role for the administrator of company 5140 to manage users belonging to company 5140.
Setup:

  • Business Role ID: ZBRS_5140_ADMINISTRATOR
  • The values ​​of Access Categories are Restricted.
  • The values of User Group field value are ZCB5140_USER. User Group is Leading Restriction.
  • Other field values are blank.

I think the User Group field supports displaying or changing user data.

The administrator with the role ZBRS_5140_ADMINISTRATOR can do the following (based on what I've tried):

  • View all user data on the system (even if those users are not in User Group ZCB5140_USER)

 

  • Edit user data belonging to User Group ZCB5140_USER
  • Cannot edit user data belonging to other User Groups

Question:

  • Why can this administrator view all user data on the system (even if those users are not in User Group ZCB5140_USER)
  • Is my role setup correct?

Thank you very much.

QuocDT5

entry.title: Automatic sales order creation
entry.content:
Olá,

Gostaria de entender o processo para criar automaticamente um pedido de venda a partir de um contrato de venda de serviços. Tenho tentado, mas não obtive sucesso.

Qual seria a melhor abordagem para gerar automaticamente o pedido de venda e a fatura mensal a partir de um contrato de venda de serviços?

Cenário no SAP Cloud Public Edition.
